_doc:
Phase 10 demo — IR → circuitJSON & component inference.

This is the SCENE produced by importing the hand-written 2:1 MUX file at `examples/hdl-corpus/phase8-mux2-handwritten.v` through the full import pipeline:

  text  →  parseVerilog (Phase 8)  →  AST
        →  elaborate (Phase 9)      →  IR
        →  toCircuit (Phase 10)    →  this circuit.json

What Phase 10 ships:
  • Ports (input/output/inout) → INPUT / OUTPUT / CLOCK nodes (the importer recognises `clk` / `clock` / `tck` as a clock).
  • Primitive instances (`and g(y, a, b);` etc.) → GATE_SLOT nodes with the matching `gate` prop.
  • Sequential always blocks with one NBA (with or without an async-reset if-guard) → REGISTER node, with DATA / CLR / CLK pins wired automatically.
  • Sub-module instantiations → SUB_CIRCUIT placeholder with the IR preserved on `_verilog` (the inner module won't be re-elaborated until Phase 10b).
  • Continuous assigns we can't simplify (`assign y = a ^ b;`, `assign s = sel ? a : b;`) → VERILOG_BLOCK node carrying the IR fragment, so the original Verilog text round-trips out untouched.

What's NOT yet here (Phase 11+):
  • Auto-layout — every imported node ships at (0,0) for now; opening this scene shows them stacked. Phase 11 routes them onto a grid.
  • Drag-and-drop import UX — Phase 12 wires a modal with file-picker + live preview.

Run `node examples/tests/test-hdl-toCircuit.mjs` for the unit suite (28 checks).
